text: Following a laparoscopic right colectomy for cecal cancer, the surgeon performs an isoperistaltic anastomosis. Two enterotomies are made for the insertion of a linear stapler. A suspension stitch is placed to facilitate the insertion of the stapler before firing.

Following a laparoscopic right colectomy for colon cancer, the surgeon performs an isoperistaltic anastomosis. Two enterotomies are created to insert a linear stapler, and a suspension stitch is placed to facilitate the stapler's insertion before firing.
The surgeon uses the suspension stitch to close the enterocolotomy, applying two layers of running absorbable sutures to secure the closure.
The surgeon uses the suspension stitch to close the entrocolotomy, applying two layers of running absorbable sutures to secure the closure.
The surgeon completes the running suture, bringing it back to the initial starting point, ensuring closure of the surgical site.
The surgeon completes the running suture by tying it off at the initial starting point, securing the closure of the surgical site.
